lesson.menu_desc: The language of clauses and obligations -- shall / must / be required to -- 28 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 14 -- Reading Contracts -- The Santos Coffee Contract
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Walyson Ginaldo Silva | Aula 14 -- Reading Contracts

listenings.text: Hello Walyson, this is Sofia from the legal team. I have read the Santos coffee contract and I want to walk you through the key clauses before you sign. First, the obligation clause: the seller shall deliver the green coffee by the effective date, and the buyer must pay within thirty days. Second, the warranty clause: the seller warrants that the coffee meets the premium grade. Third, please look at the liability clause. If either party fails to comply with the contract, that party is in breach and is required to indemnify the other side for any loss. The contract is binding once both parties sign. Note the expiry date at the end, and remember the governing law is Brazilian law. Call me back if any clause is not clear, and we will review it together before you sign.

This is my review note for the Santos coffee contract. My goal is to read every clause carefully before I sign. The obligation clause is clear: I shall deliver the coffee by the effective date, and the buyer must pay within thirty days. I checked the warranty clause, and I can confirm the premium grade. The liability clause worries me a little: if I am in breach, I am required to indemnify the buyer, so I must comply with every term. The contract is binding once we both sign, so I will not sign until every clause is correct. I also confirmed the expiry date and the governing law. If one clause is not clear, I will call Sofia from the legal team before I sign anything.

extra_audio.text: First, read the whole contract slowly and find the main clauses. Next, check the obligation clause to see what each party shall and must do. After that, read the warranty and the liability clause to know who is responsible if there is a breach. Then, confirm the effective date, the expiry date, and the governing law. Finally, ask the legal team about any clause that is not clear, and only sign when the contract is correct and binding.
